title: Planet Labs PBC Stock Analysis - Growth and Profitability Metrics
text:
Planet Labs PBC demonstrates strong growth and improving profitability metrics that position the company as a leader in Earth observation. The company achieved revenues growing at an average rate of 18.9% per year, with forecast growth of 23.9% per annum. Earnings are forecast to grow 33.1% per annum, with EPS growth expected at 31.7% per annum.

Fiscal 2026 results show the company reached its first full year of non-GAAP profitability with adjusted EBITDA of $15.5 million, compared to losses in prior years. Free cash flow surged to positive $52.9 million from negative $64 million previously, demonstrating the company's ability to convert growth into operating cash flow. The non-GAAP gross margin of 59% for the full year supports the view that the company has achieved sustainable profitability.

The company's backlog of $900 million represents substantial revenue visibility, with approximately 85% of revenue derived from annual or multi-year contracts. This recurring revenue model provides predictability for investors and reduces execution risk. The company is forecast to grow earnings and revenue by 33.1% and 23.9% per annum respectively, with positive momentum expected to continue.

Defense and Intelligence segment growth exceeding 50% year-over-year signals strong demand from government customers. Looking ahead, fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $415-440 million represents continued acceleration in the growth trajectory. The combination of strong demand, improving profitability, and substantial backlog support analyst consensus Buy ratings with average price targets around $35.
